reviews.review_description: It’s a very fun experience like it’s really hard and fun at the same time. It’s it’s harder than the first game and it’s longer so it’s a very very interesting interesting experience. I recommend every every person who plays VR should play it. ￼

It’s so fun and the best thing is it’s challenging but not too difficult, which is often the problem with most puzzle games, either they are too easy or too hard, but Floor Plan 2 is a perfect in-between. Alongside it’s wacky adventures and puzzles is an amazingly goofy and funny aesthetic and design! I especially love the side quest of collecting the little Red Harrys, they’re so cute!
Overall, I think the devs have really outdone themselves on this perfectly wacky and unique adventure game. So 5 stars and highly recommended for anyone searching for a fun puzzle game to get your brain thinking. 
Also… We need a Floor Plan 3… please.
My favorate game to watch Fynnpire play, wanted it ever since till today, honestly one of the best games and never gets boring.
The game is so fun and my faite part is the hands where you can like. Change them. That’s like the best part five stars.

Ngl this game is fun and way better then the first one there is a overnight version in it so when your done with the story then you can do that you can also do the backstory dlc one I am kinda upset there were less floors in floor plan 2 then floor plan 1 10/10 would recommend
Finally a game that's cost equals its entertainment, floor plan 2 is a hilariously fun game for any age with challenging puzzles and fun side quest missions. Love it!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing, and sorry for the frustration! The ghost doesn't need to reappear to possess the tourist - there are some other things you might need to do first. We have a walkthrough available at our help site (http://help.elevator.how), and we're also happy to help directly if that doesn't work or you need any more assistance. You reach out to us at hello@turbo-button.com.

This game needs to be more hyped. Had pretty low expectations going in, having not heard anything about it and it ended up being one of my favorite gaming experiences ever, let alone VR experiences. The world, the charm, the mechanics are all 10/10. If you at all enjoy solving puzzles/escape rooms and want to be transported to a silly wonderful trippy cartoony world get this game and don’t look back. (Also I’m really sensitive to motion sickness and this game has absolutely zero of that)

it’s THE GOAT THE GOAT i mean it’s THE GOAT

Floor Plan 1 feels like a demo compared to this. There are tons of more things to do and places to see, the muppet aesthetic is fun, and nothing feels too frustrating EDIT: The west tower is the worst thing ever. These aren't puzzles but random sets of illogical BS.  Having to give a thumbs up all the time is a little annoying, but that's my only complaint
Alot of fun but like most vr puzzle games half the most "difficulty" puzzles are the ones were i had no idea that a mechanic needed to solve it even exists

We weren't all little boys whose parents bought us Atari's, PS1's or Xbox machines. This game is NOT EASY FOR SOMEONE NEW TO VIDEO GAMING - it's a HASSLE. You have to YouTube the video step-by-steps all the time. The "Hints" the elevator gives you is honestly not enough (they're super cryptic)
It'd be WAAAAY more helpful for the elevator tips to tell you what floor / what to touch instead of the cryptic messages it delivers. 
Coming from a woman who was told to NOT play video games growing up, this game is NOT easy for first time gamers (unless you want to take off your headset every 3 minutes to figure out wtf to do by watching YouTube videos for the 1 minute challenge you're in) 
They should let the experienced gamer people do the game without tips, should they choose, and the elevator tips be for dummy's, like me, who haven't done video games puzzles before (TELL ME WHAT TO PRESS/WHERE TO PUT IT)
Honestly, if you want a fun game and have little to no gaming experience, this isn't the game for you - it's for people who have played puzzle games / online games for years.

Here's a game with puzzles, objects and trying to figure out solution to progress. It's pretty much similar to adventure games. There's no movement, just teleporting to fixed positions.

I like the style, weird looking characters and gibberish they talk. There's pretty clever puzzles and it will be a good challenge to figure them out. Whever you get stuck you can ask hints from operator to help you advance. I feel it's so rare to have hint system in game but this one has and i really appreciated it. I had to spend way less time looking solutions from internet. Duration of the game, while short was still plenty fun and the dlc added more. Speaking of dlc backside story, i feel puzzles in that were even more clever than in main game. Whole game had this weirdness about it that kept me interested till the end.

Shortness can be a flaw, whole game can be finished in only few hours if you're good at puzzles or following a walkthrough. I would have preferred to have free movement in the world, teleport system and how things were placed in each screen meant i was constantly at wrong spot and had to adjust my position irl. You can grab stuff from far but it felt janky, sometimes i couldn't make it recognize that there was item to pick or hands went all crazy because tracking issues. Not necessarily a bad thing but humor in this felt it's meant for younger audience.

Overall this was one of better vr games i've played and i enjoyed it. If you like puzzle adventure games i recommend it, you won't be disappointed.
I was in the the west tower on floor two with flys on my hand but the frog wouldn’t give me the antenna but the rest was fun

reviews.review_title: I love how fun & creative this game is!
reviews.review_description: What a breath of fresh air! I’m tired of stressful, fast-paced games where you all you do is kill stuff. This game is a sweet change from that. Also, you can tell that the developers have put a lot of thought in creating and desiging the small details of this game. The graphics and the overall quality is top notch. I wasn’t expecting it too be this good to be honest. Really recommend it 👍
Very fun game get it now

I just finished The Room VR and I was looking for something to scratch that puzzle itch. Then, I saw this game in my discovery page of the store and I knew I had to get this. Each elevator features hand-crafted puzzles that are incredibly fun to solve. There are a lot of “Ah-ha!” moments and the solutions are not too obtuse either. If you’re stuck, you can always ask for help, and the clues can be as vague or direct as you want them to be. If you like puzzle games (or cool environments and interesting characters) then this game is for you!

I didn’t know what I was expecting but this game

developer_response.body: Oh no! We haven't heard of this issue before. You can try turning snap turn on and off in the options menu, and see if that helps, but please reach out to us (hello@turbo-button.com) if the issue persists!

This game has been my favorite since it came out in 2021.. ever since I finished it I was waiting for the next one and it’s finally here! I just finished the new one and it exceeded all of my expectations and it’s a spectacular extension, one thing I would change is if you drop something it should spawn back in the elevator and not just stay on the floor. I know it makes the game more realistic, but sometimes I don’t notice that I dropped it instead of putting it in my pouch and then when I need it I have to search all the floors and rooms to find it. Its asks you for a thumbs up if you can hear her and yes we can hear her and put the tumbs up but it never works. Have restarted the game numerous times and same thing happens. What a waste of money on this.

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear this! Try to make sure your thumb is not resting on the top of the controller (or touching the thumbstick or touchpad) and that you are squeezing both the grip and the trigger. The buttons on many controllers are touch-sensitive and so the game doesn’t think your thumb is up when you are resting it on the controller, even if it is not pressing a button. If you're still stuck, we're happy to help further at hello@turbo-button.com.

developer_response.body: Any items you leave behind should reappear next to where you left them, or on a surface like the bellhop's desk or booths in the carnival. reviews.review_description: amazing game highly recommend if you're into puzzles
Not my type of puzzle game. There is little logic behind most of the puzzles, and they're extremely linear (need to do A before B before C) pretty lame

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review. We hope you'll come back and check out the free new elevator we recently added in the Backside Story update.

reviews.review_title: Starts amazing, falls apart at end
reviews.review_description:
The first act is brilliant. I wish it carried on for the whole game with that format. Truly clever solves and hilarious dialogue. 

Unfortunately the puzzles require more and more logic leaps as the game progresses until the final boss which is so poorly clued and implemented (and bewilderingly nonsensical) I almost quit. 

There were numerous opportunities where the devs clearly should have added some scenting to what was happening (why can’t I grab this? Why can’t I put A into B?) or where in game clues were irrelevant (hinting as though I had accomplished something I hadn’t gotten to yet). I also encountered a couple bugs where an event wouldn’t trigger until I restarted the section - which meant I never knew later if things weren’t working due to poor state management in the software, or if I hadn’t figured something out yet. 

I think they ran out of time and rushed stuff out with minimal play testing and refinement. 

That said, that first act is one of my favorite bits of gaming in my 35 years of gaming. I was laughing hysterically and seriously impressed with the creativity. I just wish they let this bake in the oven for a few more months. 

And kudos to the dialogue writers. It’s perfect.
Enjoyed this escape room style game!

I get that it's supposed to be silly - but the puzzles are beyond silly.  Some are fairly obvious but many are just a case of madly trying every supid thing you can think of until something works.  If you want puzzles that make sense - this is NOT the game for you.
I finished the game in on hour just to realise that there is nothing else to do.( there are only three levels)
My room is not tall enough to get the glasses in east tower so 3 stars

The game was good but every time I got to floor 3 it wouldn’t let me move so I had to restart the game and it didn’t load. Also I can’t refund it waste of money! And also the game doesn’t always work with the thumbs

developer_response.body:
Sorry to hear about this issue! We haven't heard of bugs that prevent players from accessing the 3rd floor, but we'd like to look into this. We're happy to help if you reach out to us at hello@turbo-button.com.

For the thumbs up / down detection - please make sure your thumb is completely lifted off the surface of the controller when making the gesture. It won't detect if your thumb rests on the buttons. Sorry for the inconvenience!

reviews.review_description:
I've only just started playing and immediately have issues playing it. Lack of movement with analog stick is troublesome. Small playing space? Disability? Forget about playing this game comfortably or at all. 
Rest of the game so far is great, but those limited controls are highly lowering the joy of playing.

Devs, please! Add analog stick movement and you will have near perfect game!
when you get to the part when you start the game and it says checking 1 2 3 and you have to put up a thumbs up or down it wont let me put either of them up

developer_response.body: Thanks for playing Floor Plan 2. To make the thumbs up or thumbs down gesture, please make sure your thumb is raised off the controller (not touching the thumbstick or buttons) while squeezing the grip and index finger triggers. The Oculus Quest controllers have touch sensitive surfaces and will not register that gesture if your thumb is resting anywhere on the controller. We've had a couple other players report this issue and that fixed it for them, but if this doesn't work, please email us at hello@turbo-button.com and we can troubleshoot this further.

reviews.review_description: I don’t usually review, but I had to with this one. I just upgraded from the Go to the Quest 2, and having played Floor Plan 1, it was a no brainer to try 2. I can’t believe how amazing this game is! It’s funny, the puzzles are great and if you like 1, you’ll love 2. Stop reading and get it now!
